data.summary: Experience the vibrant contrast of Tokyo's neon skyline and Kyoto's serene temples, perfect for summer exploration and scenic mountain hiking.

tips: August is extremely hot and humid; prioritize morning hikes to avoid the midday heat.
Many traditional restaurants in Kyoto offer excellent vegetarian shojin-ryori set meals.
Stay hydrated and use 'Konbini' (convenience stores) for cheap, quick vegetarian snacks.
Learn basic Japanese phrases; while Tokyo is English-friendly, rural hiking trails may not be.
Book your Shinkansen tickets in advance if travelling during the Obon festival period.
data.visaInfo: New Zealand citizens can visit Japan for up to 90 days for tourism purposes without a visa under the visa waiver agreement.
data.bestTimeToVisit: Spring (late March-April) for cherry blossoms or Autumn (November) for fall foliage; however, August is fantastic for vibrant summer festivals like Gion Matsuri.
